description: This dual-axis line chart presents the computed composition dependence of Vickers hardness (H_V) and Debye temperature (Θ_D) for the B2-structured Fe_{1-x}Mn_xAl alloy system from x = 0 (FeAl) to x = 1.0 (MnAl). Red squares connected by lines denote H_V referenced to the left y-axis (Hardness / GPa), while blue squares denote Θ_D referenced to the right y-axis (Debye temperature / K). Both parameters exhibit a strongly correlated non-monotonic trend: they decrease sharply from FeAl toward a minimum near the equiatomic Fe_{0.5}Mn_{0.5}Al composition, subsequently increase around x ≈ 0.75, and then decline again toward MnAl. Visual inspection indicates that FeAl possesses the highest values (H_V ≈ 16.5 GPa, Θ_D ≈ 653 K), whereas MnAl shows the lowest values (H_V ≈ 5 GPa, Θ_D ≈ 504 K). The synchronous variation supports the conclusion that interatomic bonding strength, particularly covalent character reflected by the Debye temperature, governs the hardness evolution across the series. Consequently, FeAl exhibits the strongest covalent bonding and highest stiffness, while increased Mn content weakens covalent character and reduces hardness, consistent with the reported B/G ratios and elastic anisotropy indices.
